behaviors.description: The speech-to-text output preserves speaker labels so that the summary can attribute statements to specific attendees.
behaviors.justification: The failure boundary is the real speech-to-text model’s diarization/label quality. A stubbed transcript would hide label-loss or speaker-mix errors. Not Workflow because this behavior targets one model output property (speaker labels), not multi-agentic stage correctness.
behaviors.test_description: Run transcription on real meeting audio with known speakers via the real STT API and assert speaker labels are present and consistently attached to utterances used downstream for attribution.

behaviors.description: Transcript parsing extracts explicit task assignments (assignee + task text) into the summary input structure.
behaviors.justification: This is deterministic parsing/structuring logic on transcript text. The behavior text does not require real network, subprocess, or model judgment; module-local parser failures are catchable in-process.
behaviors.test_description: Feed parser function transcript snippets containing explicit assignments and assert extracted objects contain correct assignee and task text fields.
behaviors.plan_consistent_note: Higher levels rejected because behavior is parser logic, not external dependency behavior.

behaviors.description: The live summary output contains at least one action item for every attendee who was assigned a task during the meeting.
behaviors.justification: Correctness depends on real LLM output decisions (coverage and attendee mapping). Deterministic stubs can verify plumbing but cannot reveal missed/omitted action items from model behavior. Not Workflow because it checks one live summarization outcome criterion, not a full multi-step agent pipeline contract.
behaviors.test_description: Send real transcript inputs with known per-attendee assignments to the production summarization model and assert each assigned attendee appears in at least one action item in live output.

behaviors.description: Decision extraction logic routes identified decisions into the `decisions` section schema rather than generic narrative fields.
behaviors.justification: Behavior text focuses on routing logic after decisions are identified. This is schema-mapping logic that can fail within local code boundaries without real infra or model variability.
behaviors.test_description: Given pre-identified decision entities, test routing/mapping function and assert outputs populate `decisions` schema fields and not narrative buckets.
behaviors.plan_consistent_note: Agentic rejected because behavior is routing of already-identified decisions, not model quality of identifying them.

behaviors.description: Key decisions mentioned in the transcript appear in the live summary's `decisions` section, not buried in the narrative.
behaviors.justification: This evaluates semantic extraction/placement quality of the real LLM output. Mocked outputs would trivially pass and hide failures where the model narrates decisions but omits structured decision entries.
behaviors.test_description: Use real meeting transcripts containing clear decisions, run live summarization with the real LLM, and assert those decisions are present in the `decisions` section rather than only free-text narrative.

behaviors.description: When the audio file is longer than 60 minutes, the service splits it into segments and processes them sequentially, merging the results into a single coherent summary.
behaviors.justification: This is explicitly multi-step orchestration across repeated model calls (segment STT/summarization) plus merge/coherence across stages. Single-step tests miss handoff and accumulation failures between steps.
behaviors.test_description: Run a real >60-minute recording through production segmentation flow, verify ordered per-segment processing occurred, and assert final merged summary is structurally complete and cross-segment coherent.

behaviors.description: The full pipeline (upload audio, transcribe, summarize, deliver structured output) completes without manual intervention for a real meeting recording.
behaviors.justification: Behavior is end-to-end pipeline success over multiple stages including real STT and LLM calls plus delivery. Real failures emerge at stage handoffs/retries/state propagation; lower levels cannot validate this complete chain.
behaviors.test_description: Execute full production-like flow on a real meeting recording: upload, transcription, summarization, and output delivery; assert completion status, valid structured schema, and no manual recovery steps.
